hero.title: HR Director Summary
hero.description: Your people-and-business correlation lens on VOIS — moving HR reporting from flat data side-by-side to 'why' and 'so what'. Track top-talent glide path, headcount with efficiency-initiative overlay, end-to-end contract → VRF → hire, Spirit Beat drivers, and L&D effectiveness against business outcomes — all narrated by AI commentary across service towers and markets.

crossFunctionalHR.narrative: CARE shows the clearest cause-and-effect pattern this month: L&D adoption at 61% (−19pp vs avg) coincides with a Spirit Beat of 71 (24% detractors) and a softer NPS of +28 with persistent Tier-3 backlog. By contrast, D&IT combines 92% L&D adoption, Spirit 89 and Copilot 78% with a 34% rise in AI-productivity outcome. Attrition is up in I/O — investigate against in-flight refresh and Passive concentration (41%).

initiatives.name: Vendor consolidation
initiatives.desc: Profit lever · Q2-25 · Owner: COO · Realised Sep-25

initiatives.name: Platform AI rollout
initiatives.desc: Profit lever · H1-26 · Owner: CTO · Forecast Jun-26

initiatives.name: Tier-3 automation
initiatives.desc: Profit lever · H2-26 · Owner: Service · Forecast Sep-26

talentDemand.dealMeta: Gen-2 deal · signed Feb-26 · €94M · requires 1,000 hires in 3 months

drivers.name: Work-Life Balance
drivers.desc: Strongest positive driver this cycle · flexible-working policy rollout cited 64% of comments.

drivers.name: Health Benefits
drivers.desc: Positive · Q3-25 benefits refresh tracks with movement in CARE & I/O.

drivers.name: Career Growth Opps.
drivers.desc: Medium · L&D adoption a partial proxy · gap in CARE.

drivers.name: Compensation
drivers.desc: Weak / flat · not a driver of current movement at population level.

aiSummary.title: HR Executive Summary
aiSummary.overall: Overall people health is resilient with two clear focus areas. Top-talent glide path tracks on plan, Spirit Beat continues to rise, and L&D investment is producing measurable productivity correlation in D&IT. The pressure points are concentrated in CARE and around contract-driven hiring on Project Atlas.

takeaways.text: Promotions: 24.6% YTD vs 25% annual glide path — on plan.
Spirit Beat 85 (▲ 3) — work-life balance the strongest correlated driver.
L&D ↔ Productivity: +0.82 correlation with AI case volume; time-to-productivity down 4 days.

watchpoints.text: CARE · DE hotspot: promotion 18.2% (−6.4pp), L&D 61%, Spirit 71 — intervention required.
Project Atlas hiring: 163 of 1,000 hires made (16%) — on glide path but bottlenecks in VRF approval queue.
I/O attrition ▲ during platform refresh; cross-check with Passive concentration.

actions.text: Tower-level intervention plan for CARE · DE with COO and L&D lead.
Unblock VRF approval queue for Project Atlas — target +50 offers / week.
Run targeted Glint pulse on I/O ahead of next cycle.

HR.1.title: HR.1 — Cross-Functional HR View
HR.1.status: PARTIALLY COVERED · NARRATIVE GAP TO BUILD
HR.1.description: Correlated HR storytelling (LLM-based AI commentary) that links HR metrics (L&D adoption, training, Spirit Beat and NPS) to business performance per service tower — to answer 'why' and 'so what', not just present flat data sets side by side.
HR.1.acceptanceCriteria: For a selected service tower / market, the user sees HR metrics on one page with a narrative explaining likely cause-and-effect (e.g. low L&D in area X correlates with low Spirit score).
HR.1.userStory: As the HR Director, I want correlated HR storytelling (AI-generated commentary) that links L&D adoption, training, Spirit Beat and NPS to business performance per service tower, so that I can answer the 'why' and 'so what' rather than read flat data sets side by side.
feedback: Surfaces correlations across learning plans and engagement outcomes
Refreshed dynamically based on monthly closes

HR.1.1.title: HR.1.1 — Efficiency → Headcount Linkage
HR.1.1.description: Efficiency-initiative overlay on the existing headcount actual / forecast view, mapping strategic profitability initiatives and cost take-out to the resulting headcount movement.
HR.1.1.acceptanceCriteria: User opens the headcount page and sees actuals and forecast with a layer showing which efficiency initiatives drove (or are forecast to drive) each material movement.
HR.1.1.userStory: As the HR Director, I want an efficiency-initiative overlay on the existing headcount actual/forecast view that maps strategic profitability initiatives and cost take-out to the resulting headcount movement, so that I understand the 'why' behind every material headcount change.
feedback: FTE reductions mapped to specific project launches
Initiatives overlaid directly on Recharts headcount line chart

HR.1.2.title: HR.1.2 — Talent Demand Outlook
HR.1.2.description: Talent demand and skills outlook view: capacity utilisation mapped against demand trends and skill requirements. Sourced from Workforce Management once the WFM transitional solution is in place. End-to-end linkage from contract / opportunity → demand → positions → hiring requests (VRFs) → SuccessFactors, so recruitment can be tracked against contract-driven demand (e.g. 1,000 hires in 3 months).
HR.1.2.acceptanceCriteria: Once WFM is live, the page shows utilisation forecast vs demand by service tower with a skills-requirement layer. When a contract is signed that requires new hires, the Hub shows the demand, the positions created, the open VRFs and the hires made — all linked back to the originating deal.
HR.1.2.userStory: As the HR Director, I want a talent demand and skills outlook view linking contract/opportunity → demand → positions → VRFs → SuccessFactors, so that I can forecast utilisation against demand and track recruitment against contract-driven demand from a single Hub view.
feedback: Linked pipeline displayed for Project Atlas as a primary use case
Gated behind Workforce Management data standardisation

HR.1.3.title: HR.1.3 — Spirit Survey 'Why' Analysis
HR.1.3.description: Spirit Beat 'why' view that correlates survey results with other business data sets based on a one-off historical analysis to identify meaningful correlations, then tracked as an ongoing framework.
HR.1.3.acceptanceCriteria: Spirit page shows trend data plus a 'likely drivers' panel listing the business signals that correlate most strongly with the latest movement, refreshed each survey cycle.
HR.1.3.userStory: As the HR Director, I want a Spirit Beat 'why' view that correlates survey results with other business data sets via a one-off historical analysis then tracks those correlations as an ongoing framework, so that I understand the likely drivers behind Spirit trends rather than rely on pockets of narrative in PowerPoints.
feedback: Drivers show positive and negative correlation scores
Updated automatically when Glint releases new cycle scores

HR.1.4.title: HR.1.4 — L&D / Training Effectiveness
HR.1.4.description: L&D effectiveness view answering 'what is the business / AI-productivity impact of our 80% AI learning-plan adoption' — correlation between training adoption and downstream business KPIs (including Copilot adoption).
HR.1.4.acceptanceCriteria: User opens the L&D page and sees training adoption against the business / productivity KPIs it is hypothesised to influence, with the correlation strength surfaced over a meaningful time window.
HR.1.4.userStory: As the HR Director, I want an L&D effectiveness view showing training adoption against the business and AI-productivity KPIs it is hypothesised to influence with correlation strength over a meaningful time window, so that I can demonstrate whether L&D investment is driving real business improvement.
feedback: Calculates Pearson r correlation scores rolling monthly
Outcomes connect training adoption with operational incident deflection

TLNT.title: Talent Metrics with Targets
TLNT.description: Top-talent promotion tracked against the annualised target with a flat month-on-month progression (~2% / month for 25% annual), with the option to drill to hotspots by line of business and geography.
TLNT.acceptanceCriteria: Top-line promotion % vs target visible with monthly progression. A drill-down view shows hotspots by LoB / location flagged red where targets are missed.
TLNT.userStory: As the HR Director, I want top-talent promotion tracked against the annualised target with flat month-on-month progression and the option to drill into hotspots by line of business and geography, so that I can see whether we are on glide path and where intervention is needed.
feedback: Glide path targets updated to FY27 values
Hotspots highlight under-performance in CARE DE
